name: Dafny
summary: Microsoft Research auto-active verification language (K. Rustan M. Leino): refinement types plus loop invariants plus pre/post conditions, all SMT-discharged via Boogie. Used at AWS for the IAM policy engine (~10 KLOC verified Dafny replacing C++), at Cloudflare for proxy authority logic, and increasingly in Linux kernel verification work. The IAM deployment is the largest production codebase verified in any refinement-types system to date.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Dafny (Microsoft Research, K. Rustan M. Leino) is the canonical auto-active verification language: refinement types plus loop invariants plus pre/post conditions, all SMT-discharged via Boogie (`data/verification-intermediate-languages`).

Used at **AWS for the IAM policy engine** (~10 KLOC of verified Dafny replacing C++), at **Cloudflare** for proxy authority logic, and increasingly in **Linux kernel verification** work. The IAM deployment is the largest production codebase verified in any refinement-types system to date.

sections.title: Auto-active verification discipline
sections.content:
*Auto-active* names the design point between *automatic* (push-button: programmer writes only the program) and *interactive* (Coq/Lean style: programmer writes the proof). Dafny asks the programmer to supply *invariants and assertions* but not full proofs — the SMT solver discharges the obligations once invariants are strong enough.

This is the sweet spot for production verification: programmers learn to write loop invariants and post-conditions but never have to manipulate proof terms. The IAM deployment confirms the discipline scales: 10 KLOC of business logic verified by engineers who are software developers, not formal-methods specialists.

sections.title: Production deployments and the IAM lesson
